title: Earliest Recoverable Source Witness Audit Checklist
purpose: Ensure the restored/source-audited scripture layer is aligned with the earliest recoverable textual witnesses, not merely with a later Protestant, Catholic, Orthodox, or modern denominational translation tradition.
core_principles: Use the familiar 66-book scaffold as a working build order only, not as a claim of earliest canon boundary.
Prioritize earliest recoverable textual witnesses and source languages wherever possible.
Keep source-audited restored translation separate from mystical/contemplative companion layer.
Preserve uncertainty honestly where textual witnesses differ.
Avoid anti-Jewish, supersessionist, anti-body, misogynistic, anti-queer, or domination-sanctioning readings where the source text does not require them.
After initial book completion, run deeper source-witness audit passes for variant-sensitive books/passages.
source_witnesses_to_track: Septuagint / Old Greek traditions where relevant
Samaritan Pentateuch for Torah where relevant
Greek New Testament textual tradition and major manuscript variants
Early versions such as Syriac, Latin, Coptic where variant-sensitive
Second Temple and Septuagintal/deuterocanonical expansion texts after core scaffold
second_pass_tasks: Review completed books for stronger source-witness metadata.
Flag major textual variants already known in completed New Testament books.
Flag major Hebrew/Greek/Samaritan/Dead Sea Scroll differences in completed Hebrew Bible books.
Add Septuagint/deuterocanonical expansion track after core scaffold reaches stable coverage.
Add Second Temple wisdom/visionary track after canonical/deuterocanonical scaffold is stable.
working_note: The project remains aligned around earliest recoverable source integrity: build the restored layer first, preserve mystical companion separately, then enrich with deeper witness audit metadata and expansion tracks.
